items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, representing significant investment in San José's commercial corridor. This is the only non-residential project in this permit batch and could bring new business activity to the area.

items.headline: City commissions set priorities for next fiscal year
items.summary: Saratoga City Council approved work plans from five commissions covering heritage preservation, library services, public art, parks and recreation, and traffic safety for FY 2026-27. The approved plans will guide which projects and initiatives each commission focuses on over the coming year.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering an amnesty program to help businesses operating in shopping centers get licensed, paired with outreach efforts and summer "Shop Local" campaigns. This could affect local small businesses and retail establishments seeking to formalize their operations.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up designated as annual city event
items.summary: The City Council approved the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual City-sponsored special event on March 25, 2026. The city will allocate funds and accept donations of materials and services to support the cleanup effort.

items.headline: City Council reviews federal community development grant applications
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed funding applications for Community Development Block Grants on March 25, which provide federal money for affordable housing and local services. The outcome will determine which local projects receive this competitive federal funding.

items.headline: Palo Alto updates zoning and building rules for retail vitality
items.summary: The Planning Commission is considering amendments to zoning and building codes to implement retail vitality policies from the city's Comprehensive Plan. This could affect how retail spaces are developed and regulated across Palo Alto.

items.headline: San Antonio Road area planning study underway
items.summary: The Commission is holding a study session on land use and transportation alternatives for the San Antonio Road area. This signals potential future changes to how this area is zoned or developed.

items.headline: Campbell updates beekeeping rules with new ordinance
items.summary: The city council adopted an updated beekeeping ordinance on March 24, which will change regulations for residents keeping bees. The changes affect what Campbell residents can do with backyard beekeeping operations.
